Test Description: Negative edge interrupt enable and handling per GPIO[8:39] with group and sysreg interaction
Remarks: Uses external pad control at 0xA0243ffc to drive edges; int_pend handshake between main and ISR
Test Steps / Procedure: 1) Initialize: test_err=0; conditionally enable GIC IRQ 87/88 based on GPIO0/GPIO1 macros; enable LSS sysreg interrupt (MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 with LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIOx_INTR) per macro; 2) Drive pads high: write_reg(0xA0243ffc,0xffffffff); 3) Configure per-pin control for all 32 pins: for i=0..31, addr1=MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8+(i*4); write_reg(addr1,(1<<20)|(1<<18)|(1<<16)); wait_on(10) each; 4) For i=0..31 loop: set wr_val=(1<<i); clear raw status: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1,wr_val); enable group per-pin: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1,wr_val); wait_on(10); set int_pend=1; prepare high then generate negedge: write_reg(0xA0243ffc,0xffffffff); wait_on(30); write_reg(0xA0243ffc,~wr_val); 5) Poll for ISR completion: timeout=5000; while(int_pend && timeout--) wait_on(10); if timeout==0: log timeout error and test_err++; 6) Complete: finish(test_err); ISR: Default_IRQHandler(): set local_wr=(1<<i); set int_pend=0; re-drive pads high write_reg(0xA0243ffc
